concepts.finding: AutoRec: autoencoder framework for collaborative filtering. Input: partially observed user rating vector (u-AutoRec) or item rating vector (i-AutoRec). Encoder projects to low-dimensional bottleneck, decoder reconstructs all ratings. i-AutoRec outperforms u-AutoRec. Simple but effective: beats SVD and RBM-CF on MovieLens and Netflix.
concepts.implication: Keyword embedding via autoencoder: input keyword co-occurrence vector -> encoder compresses to embedding -> decoder reconstructs co-occurrence. Denoising version (corrupt input) improves robustness. Simple, scalable baseline.
concepts.open_question: How does autoencoder-based keyword embedding compare to word2vec (CBOW/Skip-gram) for intent clustering? Are denoising autoencoders more robust?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Autoencoder-based keyword embeddings (i-AutoRec style) produce comparable intent clusters to word2vec embeddings while being simpler to train and tune.
